title: Fresh Domain Synthetic Identity with Graduated Spend Pattern
narrative: A synthetic identity is established using a newly registered email domain (P_emaildomain created <90 days ago) paired with a consistent card fingerprint. The fraudster builds legitimacy over 14-21 days through graduated spending: initial transactions of $15-$45 at low-risk MCCs (grocery, gas stations) with 100% AVS/CVV match rates. After 8-12 successful small transactions, velocity increases to 3-5 purchases per day at $80-$200 each, targeting electronics merchants (ProductCD=W). The card1 shows zero prior history before this email pairing. The bust-out phase occurs days 18-23 with 6-9 high-value transactions ($400-$850 each) within 36 hours, often targeting different merchant categories than the seasoning phase. R_emaildomain frequently matches P_emaildomain, both being disposable domains with no digital footprint. Address information (addr1) remains constant but dist1 values show illogical geographic patterns inconsistent with stated billing location.
indicators: P_emaildomain registration timestamp <90 days from first transaction date
Initial 8-12 transactions range $15-$45 with 100% AVS_CVV match, followed by 6-9 transactions $400-$850 within 36 hours
card1_txn_count_prior = 0 at account opening, card1_amt_zscore jumps from <0.5 to >3.2 during bust-out phase
P_emaildomain = R_emaildomain in 85%+ of transactions, both domains have zero search engine presence
Graduated spend pattern: 14-21 days of small purchases before velocity spike to 3-5 daily transactions
dist1 values show >500km variance despite constant addr1, inconsistent with normal card usage patterns
MCC switches from 5411/5541 (grocery/gas) in seasoning phase to 5732/5944 (electronics) during exploitation
summary: A card with no prior history appears alongside a brand-new email domain, making small legitimate-looking purchases (groceries, gas) for two weeks before suddenly switching to rapid high-value electronics purchases within 36 hours. The billing address stays the same but distance patterns are geographically impossible.
reasoning: Fraudsters create a fake identity and deliberately build a clean payment history to bypass fraud filters, then cash out with maximum purchases once trust is established. The fresh domain, zero card history, and abrupt shift from trust-building to aggressive spending reveal orchestrated abuse rather than a real customer's evolving needs.
indicator_explanations: Email domain was created very recently, just before the account opened
Small test purchases establish legitimacy before jumping to large fraudulent amounts
Card shows no history initially, then spending explodes beyond normal range
Shipping and billing emails match but both domains are invisible online
Account builds trust with weeks of normal purchases before attacking
Location data wildly inconsistent despite address staying the same throughout
Shopping shifts from everyday necessities to high-value resellable electronics
